summary:
Held 5 of today's 10 biggest US movers.

• 2 upside misses — ROIV +15%, DFH +11%. These cleared the universe screen but no strategy picked them up; the morning brief either filtered them on a catalyst/RSI/earnings rule or had nothing positive to trigger an entry.

• 3 downside moves (HAS -9%, NTNX -7%, DUOL -6%) are unreachable on a long-only basket — no mechanism to express a short or pair-trade.

• Most instructive miss: ROIV (+14.89%) — Q4 FY2026 EPS beat: $0.28 vs. -$0.35 est. (boosted by $770M Moderna litigation settlement); brepocitinib granted Breakthrough Therapy Designation for cutaneous sarcoidosis and positive IMVT-1402 RA pipeline data. Why we passed: Earnings-day gap ~15% was fully priced pre-market before strategies could act; news-reactive signal arrived stale at open. Momentum and mean-reversion filters almost certainly rejected the name on gap-too-large or RSI-overbought criteria at the first printable bar.

top_movers.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 EPS beat: $0.28 vs. -$0.35 est. (boosted by $770M Moderna litigation settlement); brepocitinib granted Breakthrough Therapy Designation for cutaneous sarcoidosis and positive IMVT-1402 RA pipeline data.
top_movers.miss_reason: Earnings-day gap ~15% was fully priced pre-market before strategies could act; news-reactive signal arrived stale at open. Momentum and mean-reversion filters almost certainly rejected the name on gap-too-large or RSI-overbought criteria at the first printable bar.
top_movers.suggestion: Configure news-reactive to pre-flag earnings-day names with scheduled binary pipeline catalysts; allow open-auction entry within 15 min of open on volume ≥2× 20-day avg if gap is +8–20%, bypassing the standard gap-exclusion filter on scheduled catalyst dates.

top_movers.catalyst: ENPH opened U.S. pre-orders for its IQ9S-3P commercial three-phase microinverter; customers can safe-harbor equipment ahead of federal tax credit deadlines — drove a ~13% single-session spike.
top_movers.miss_reason: ENPH had already surged ~60% from late-April lows before today, likely pushing RSI past momentum-trader's overbought gate; news-reactive likely filtered it out as a product pre-order press release doesn't match its earnings/M&A catalyst patterns.
top_movers.suggestion: In news-reactive, add a 'safe-harbor deadline' press-release tag that bypasses the RSI overbought gate in momentum-trader, allowing re-entry when a name gaps on a confirmed product pre-order tied to an imminent tax credit window.

top_movers.catalyst: Mid-quarter 8-K brokerage update reversed May 7 earnings-call guidance: RXO now expects May truckload gross profit/load ≥ April (vs. a projected decline), as spot rates hit a 4-year high at +16.5% YoY.
top_movers.miss_reason: Post-May-7 earnings gap likely pushed RSI above momentum-trader's overbought threshold. The trigger was a voluntary intra-quarter 8-K brokerage update — not a scheduled earnings event — a category news-reactive likely excludes from its tier-1 signal taxonomy.
top_movers.suggestion: Add 'intra-quarter guidance raise' as a tier-1 event type in news-reactive, keyed on 8-K filings containing keywords like 'raises outlook' or 'brokerage update', using the same directional entry logic applied to earnings-beat signals.

top_movers.catalyst: Q4 2026 earnings beat: EPS $1.89 vs $1.48 est (+28%); revenue $152M vs $131M est (+16%); $1.00/share special cash dividend declared; record FY2026 driven by strong VLGC charter rates.
top_movers.miss_reason: Earnings gap-up formed pre-market, leaving news-reactive no clean entry once the +12% move was established at open. Momentum-trader was likely blocked by overbought filters — LPG was already +71% YTD and +16.7% over 30 days, pushing RSI well into exclusion territory.
top_movers.suggestion: Add an earnings-beat release gate to news-reactive: when EPS beat ≥20% AND revenue beat ≥10%, permit a long entry at open up to a max gap of +15%, temporarily suspending the standard gap-exclusion filter for the first 15 minutes of the session.

top_movers.catalyst: CEO Ford Tamer's JP Morgan TMC Conference fireside reinforced AI-server demand; Q1 revenue beat +42% YoY to $170.9M and $1.65B AMI acquisition (announced May 4) provided the fundamental backdrop.
top_movers.miss_reason: LSCC entered today up 74% YTD and at 52-week highs, likely tripping momentum-trader's RSI/overbought filter. News-reactive cannot pre-position for an intraday conference-presentation catalyst — no actionable pre-market hard-news signal exists.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a conference-calendar scanner: for names with a prior-quarter earnings beat ≥10%, trigger a momentum-trader breakout entry on presentation days when intraday price clears prior close by ≥3% on ≥1.5× avg volume within the first 90 min.

top_movers.catalyst: Sector-wide housing rally on May 19–20 (pending home sales +3rd consecutive month, NAHB confidence ticked up May 18) lifted all homebuilder stocks ~3%; DFH outperformed on residual M&A optionality from its live hostile $704M all-cash bid for Beazer Homes.
top_movers.miss_reason: All five strategies were in DFH's universe but likely filtered it out on fundamentals: Q1 2026 EPS fell 79.6% YoY and revenue dropped 10.3%, which would have tripped control-rule-based and macro-aligned negative-earnings screens. The news-reactive strategy likely acted on the May 11 Beazer bid announcement and closed o
top_movers.suggestion: Extend news-reactive hold windows for M&A-acquirer positions to at least 7 trading days post-announcement when a hostile deal remains unresolved, so the strategy captures sector-momentum continuation rather than closing on the target's rejection alone.

top_movers.catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings: EPS beat ($1.39 vs $1.22 est.) but revenue missed ($1.0B vs $1.06B est.); cyberattack delays $40–60M of Q2 revenue to H2; FY sales guidance held at low end, disappointing investors.
top_movers.miss_reason: HAS was an earnings-day reporter; standard quant practice gates out earnings names to avoid binary gap risk. News-reactive strategies saw the revenue miss and cyberattack disclosure only post-open after the ~9% gap had already set, making the risk/reward unfavourable for a same-session entry.
top_movers.suggestion: Earnings-gate should release news-reactive for names where a revenue miss + guidance-hold produces a gap-down ≥7% confirmed within 20 min of open, permitting a short-fade entry at 0.5× standard size with a hard stop at 1.5× the opening gap.

top_movers.catalyst: Pre-earnings risk-off into May 27 Q3 print compounding Apr 7 Investor Day overhang: cautious long-term growth targets and revenue deferral from OEM/migration timing shifts continue to weigh on the stock.
top_movers.miss_reason: Pre-earnings blackout (Q3 due May 27, 7 days out) likely gated all new entries across strategies; news-reactive found no fresh same-day catalyst since the Investor Day overhang is 6 weeks stale; momentum-trader cannot short in a long-only config.
top_movers.suggestion: If short-selling is live, add a 'guidance-cut drift' rule to news-reactive: ≥10% below post-guidance-event close AND ≤7 days pre-earnings triggers a short entry at ≤20% position limit to capture sustained institutional distribution.

top_movers.catalyst: Continued post-earnings drift from May 4 Q1 results; weak Q2 bookings guidance (+6% vs ~12% expected) and FY bookings miss triggered analyst PT cuts (Evercore ISI to $97) and sustained selling amplified by ~22% short interest.
top_movers.miss_reason: In universe but excluded by all strategies: momentum-trader avoids names down 41% YTD; news-reactive's post-earnings signal decayed by day 16 after May 4; mean-reverter filtered by 22%-short-interest crowded-short risk, not a clean technical bounce setup.
top_movers.suggestion: Extend news-reactive's post-earnings signal decay window to 15 trading days for names where next-quarter bookings guidance misses consensus by >3%, capturing the guidance-miss overhang drift that materialises in weeks 2–3 post-announcement.

top_movers.catalyst: WTI crude fell >5% below $100/bbl after Trump declared US-Iran peace talks in 'final stages,' abruptly unwinding the geopolitical war-risk premium built since the Strait of Hormuz conflict began in late February 2026.
top_movers.miss_reason: After a ~60% commodity rally since Feb, momentum signal was firmly long USO; a long-only strategy cannot short a geopolitical unwind. The catalyst—Trump's surprise 'final stages' comment—had no technical pre-signal, making pre-trade positioning impossible.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a geopolitical-premium gate: when a commodity ETF has gained ≥40% in ≤90 days, tighten intraday stops to 1.5% and suppress new long entries if same-session news matches supply-disruption-resolution keywords (deal, ceasefire, Hormuz, sanctions-lifted).
